Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails for Marcus Thorne's directive about the vendor strategy memo using search_emails (query: "vendor strategy" or "Marcus Thorne" or "memo" or "backup capacity" or similar). Find: email_email_24e75fd4cfe1 (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, sent April 24 2026). Content: Marcus assigned as owner of the vendor strategy memo; Chloe's specific deliverable is a "regional backup-capacity note with practical constraints" by 1:00 PM Pacific. Also assigns Hana (Heartland dispute summary by invoice line or move), Alejandro (draft cost-impact model), and David (active-account exposure view).
- Search emails for Elena Rostova's original directive about the memo using search_emails (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "Elena Rostova" or "three options" or similar). Find: email_email_387fcebf3efd (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, sent April 24 2026). Content: formal strategy memo due Wednesday covering three options negotiate independently, replace one vendor, or split volume. Memo must include financial impact by active client account.
- Search emails for Chloe's prior vendor outreach history using search_emails (query: "backup vendor" or "corporate relocation partnership" or "exploratory" or similar) to verify whether any OTHER backup vendor besides Lakeline was probed. Find ONLY: email_email_c43077978cc3 (Chloe to sandra.walsh@lakeline-logistics.com, sent early April 2026) Chloe's confidential exploratory outreach asking about coverage corridors, rate structure, and Midwest overflow capacity. No outreach to Atlas Van Lines, Allied, Mayflower, North American Van Lines, or any other backup carrier exists in the universe. Lakeline is the only vendor Chloe probed.
- Search emails for Lakeline's reply to Chloe using search_emails (query: "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" or "Midwest" or "Southeast" or similar) or read the thread. Find: email_email_4c8116551604 (sender: sandra.walsh@lakeline-logistics.com, recipient: chloe.vance@moveops.com, sent early April 2026). KEY CONTENT: Lakeline covers "Midwest and Southeast corridors well, including IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, and parts of TX." Lakeline explicitly "cannot serve as a true national replacement on our own" NO West Coast lanes, NO international shipments. Rate advantage: 10% below current Heartland pricing. Can support a defined Midwest/Southeast overflow program.
- Search emails for the Heartland rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "rate adjustment" or "Q3" or similar). Find: email_email_aa260e7b26e7 (sender: jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com, sent April 13 2026, subject: "Formal Notice: Q3 2026 Rate Adjustment and Account Review"). KEY CONTENT: 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1 2026 (linehaul, packing, storage, shuttle, accessorial). Rate increase is EXPLICITLY TIED to the unresolved Q1 dispute on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 Jake says MoveOps must close out the dispute and align on revised pricing before the end of April to preserve preferred scheduling for summer bookings.
- Search emails for the Swift rate increase notice using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "pricing update" or "DOT documentation surcharge" or similar). Find: email_email_9a6a5e8dc032 (sender: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, sent April 11 2026, subject: "Pricing Update Effective Immediately DOT Documentation Surcharge"). KEY CONTENT: 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings + $500 per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge applied immediately. Already live on 4 in-progress shipments: Raj Patel (Dallas to Denver), Emilia Cruz (Phoenix to Portland), Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), Nora Bennett (BrightLoop).
- Search Airtable for ALL currently In Progress relocations using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base_id for Relocations base, table_name: "tblRelocations01", filter by Status = "In Progress"). Walk through each record and extract Name, Origin, Destination, Assigned Coordinator, and Special Requirements. This is a broad sweep not a targeted lookup because the prompt explicitly asks the agent to walk "every one" of the in-progress relocations.
- Retrieve per-record detail on each In Progress relocation on a route OUTSIDE Lakeline's coverage footprint (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, parts of TX). Use airtable_get_record on each record_id to confirm the route and coordinator. Exposed records to identify include at minimum: (a) Rachel Dominguez (recReloc00000000029, Los Angeles CA → Portland OR, Canopy Health, coordinator Javier Morales), (b) Kevin Okafor (recReloc00000000030, Phoenix AZ → Portland OR, Canopy Health, coordinator Blessing Okafor), (c) Maya Johansson (recReloc00000000032, Portland OR → San Diego CA, Vectral Systems, coordinator Suki Patel), (d) Joaquín Reyes (recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle, Phoenix AZ → Seattle WA, GreenStack Energy, coordinator Javier Morales), (e) Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Seattle WA → Austin TX, StormCloud Analytics, coordinator Suki Patel noting Austin TX is only "parts of TX" in Lakeline's footprint and the Seattle origin is explicitly outside Lakeline's West Coast gap), (f) Camille Oduya (recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle, Boston MA → Seattle WA, Vectral Systems, coordinator Suki Patel Northeast origin and West Coast destination both outside Lakeline), (g) Daniel Reeves (recReloc00000000031, Seattle WA → San Diego CA, Vectral Systems, coordinator Suki Patel West Coast origin and West Coast destination both outside Lakeline). Any other West Coast / Mountain / Northeast In Progress records should also be surfaced. IMPORTANT NUANCE ON "NO VENDOR LOCKED IN": the Airtable Relocations schema (tblRelocations01) does not contain a dedicated vendor field vendor assignment lives cross-service. The "no vendor locked in" inference must come from comparing each exposed record against (a) Swift's rate-notice shipment list, which names only 4 moves (Raj Patel Dallas→Denver, Emilia Cruz Phoenix→Portland, Jordan Ekwueme BrightLoop, Nora Bennett BrightLoop these 4 are already on the $500 DOT surcharge per the Swift email, NOT derived from Airtable), (b) any existing QuickBooks bill_swift_* or bill_heartland_* VendorRef entry matching the employee + route, and (c) any explicit vendor mention in the Airtable Special Requirements field. For Rachel Dominguez, Kevin Okafor, Maya Johansson, Joaquín Reyes, Jae-won Kim, and Camille Oduya: none appear on Swift's 4-shipment rate-notice list, none have a matching QuickBooks vendor bill, and none have a vendor named in Special Requirements so the vendor lock is open for each. Joaquín Reyes's Special Requirements additionally note Chloe flagged that Heartland "may not have equipment rated for 900-lb rig" with Swift as a possible alternative still not a committed vendor lock.
- Search Linear for the Heartland Q1 dispute consolidation ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "Q1" or "consolidate" or "dispute" or similar). Find: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (ticket #118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response", assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due April 15 2026, created April 14 2026). Retrieve full details using linear_get_issue. CRITICAL CONTENT the issue DESCRIPTION is where Hana's additional findings live, NOT in the QuickBooks bill line items: "Hana widened the Heartland review on April 14 after reconciling all five Q1 Heartland moves against service authorizations, coordinator notes, and invoice backup. Current findings: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves show discrepancies. Marchetti / Osei remains disputed under VRECON-2026-Q1-HM with $4,800 in invalid billing already documented. Kevin Tran file includes 2 days of storage billed without any signed contract authorization or ops addendum. Priya Nair file includes an apparent shuttle fee mismatch versus origin access notes and approved scope." Also find the earlier reconciliation ticket: issue_heartland_q1_recon (ticket #342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)", assigned to Chloe Vance, priority 2, state_in_progress, created March 25, due April 10) for baseline context on the original three disputed moves.
- Retrieve comments on linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118) using linear_list_comments for any additional specifics Hana may have added.
- Retrieve QuickBooks bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria on DocNumber: "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or vendor: "Heartland Movers") or quickbooks_get_bill. CORRECTION HM-2026-Q1-0042 is a vendor BILL (payable), not a customer invoice, and lives in quickbooks.bills.json, not quickbooks.invoices.json. Two bill records exist: bill_heartland_q1_2026 (as-submitted by Heartland, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 total, 8 lines × $1,600) and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (MoveOps-annotated hold version, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD, same $12,800 with 5 [VERIFIED] lines and 3 DISPUTED lines for Marchetti/Osei/Venkatesh). Use the bill to anchor the $12,800 total and the 8-line structure. IMPORTANT: Kevin Tran and Priya Nair do NOT appear as distinct additional-gap line items inside the bill itself Kevin Tran shows as a [VERIFIED] full-service relocation line, and Priya Nair is not present in this bill's 8 line items at all. Hana's Kevin Tran storage and Priya Nair shuttle findings are narrative discoveries documented in Linear issue #118 based on ops notes, authorization records, and coordinator notes not on new QuickBooks line items. The bill read is still needed for the $12,800 anchor and the 3-disputed-vs-5-verified structure that grounds Chloe's baseline number before layering Hana's additional findings.
- Search Linear for Alejandro's consolidated vendor margin dataset ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor margin" or "Q3 dataset" or "cost impact" or "margin" or similar). Find: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (ticket #214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM", assigned to Alejandro Fuentes, priority 1, due April 16 2026, created April 14 2026). Retrieve the issue detail using linear_get_issue. The description explicitly enumerates blockers: "Airtable vendor assignment fields are incomplete or inconsistent across active moves. QuickBooks bills are not consistently tagged to relocation records or client accounts. CRM upcoming volume is separate from operations data and difficult to map by vendor exposure." The ticket has state_id null (not Done), meaning the dataset is still In Progress and the forward-looking margin model has not yet landed as a finished artifact. This is the blocker Chloe must flag to Marcus.
- Search Slack and emails for any Alejandro updates on the margin model using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Alejandro" or "margin" or "cost impact" or "Q3 dataset" or similar) and search_emails. Confirm no landed draft exists Alejandro has only posted directional commentary (e.g., Vectral 32% blended margin, scenario analysis) but no final margin impact numbers have landed. This IS a blocker on the memo and must be flagged to Marcus.
- Search Slack for Elena's Tuesday directive in #executive and any prior vendor-crisis discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor strategy" or "Lakeline" or "Heartland" or "Swift" or "capacity" or similar) on C004 (#executive) and C006 (#operations).
- Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Also look up the coordinators on the exposed moves Javier Morales, Blessing Okafor, and Suki Patel using contacts_search_contacts to ground the coordinator-assignment findings.
- Search Linear for the vendor strategy memo tracking ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "CEO memo" or "backup capacity" or similar). Find: linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (ticket #318, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options", owner: Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due April 29 2026, created April 24 2026, status In Progress). Retrieve full details and existing comments using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments before adding a new comment.
- Send the backup-capacity note email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"], subject: short backup-capacity note title, content: body below). Content must cover: (1) Lakeline is the ONLY backup vendor Chloe probed no other outreach exists; (2) Lakeline coverage footprint (Midwest/Southeast, specific states) AND the explicit West Coast / national-replacement gap; (3) named exposed in-progress moves with coordinator assignments AND vendor-lock status inferred cross-service at minimum Rachel Dominguez (LA → Portland, Javier Morales) and Kevin Okafor (Phoenix → Portland, Blessing Okafor), both with no main vendor committed in QuickBooks bills or Swift's surcharge shipment list; (4) Heartland 20% Q3 increase explicitly tied to unresolved Q1 dispute HM-2026-Q1-0042 relevant to negotiation posture; (5) Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge already live on 4 in-progress shipments (Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz, Jordan Ekwueme, Nora Bennett); (6) Hana's ADDITIONAL Heartland Q1 findings from Linear #118 beyond the original three disputed moves documented in VRECON-2026-Q1-HM specifically: Kevin Tran has 2 days of storage billed without signed contract authorization, and Priya Nair has a shuttle fee mismatch versus origin access notes and approved scope. These are narrative findings from Hana's April 14 reconciliation review, not new QuickBooks line items, and they indicate the real exposure is broader than the $4,800 / three-move number Marcus may be working from; (7) Alejandro's cost-impact model (Linear #214) has NOT landed and remains In Progress with blockers on Airtable vendor assignment fields, QuickBooks bill tagging, and CRM volume mapping this is a blocker on the memo and Marcus needs to know now.
- Add a comment on the vendor strategy memo Linear ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, ticket #318) using linear_create_comment documenting Chloe's full findings: Lakeline coverage + hard gap, exposed moves with coordinators + vendor-lock status (inferred cross-service), Heartland Q1-dispute tie-in on rate increase, Swift already-live $500 DOT surcharge on 4 named shipments, Hana's additional Q1 findings (Kevin Tran storage + Priya Nair shuttle from Linear #118 narrative), and the Alejandro Linear #214 dataset-not-landed blocker. The comment must surface the findings so Hana, Alejandro, and David can build on them in the shared ticket.
- Post a heads-up in the #operations Slack channel (C006) using conversations_add_message. Content should call out the specific exposed in-progress moves by name (Rachel Dominguez, Kevin Okafor, any others) and their coordinators, noting that these relocations are on routes with NO vendor backstop if Heartland or Swift walks so the coordinators on those moves are aware of the vendor-risk posture while the strategy memo is being finalized.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Find: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (ticket #118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response", assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due April 15 2026, created April 14 2026). Retrieve full details using linear_get_issue. CRITICAL CONTENT the issue DESCRIPTION is where Hana's additional findings live, NOT in the QuickBooks bill line items: "Hana widened the Heartland review on April 14 after reconciling all five Q1 Heartland moves against service authorizations, coordinator notes, and invoice backup. Current findings: 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves show discrepancies. Marchetti / Osei remains disputed under VRECON-2026-Q1-HM with $4,800 in invalid billing already documented. Kevin Tran file includes 2 days of storage billed without any signed contract authorization or ops addendum. Priya Nair file includes an apparent shuttle fee mismatch versus origin access notes and approved scope." Also find the earlier reconciliation ticket: issue_heartland_q1_recon (ticket #342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)", assigned to Chloe Vance, priority 2, state_in_progress, created March 25, due April 10) for baseline context on the original three disputed moves.
- Retrieve comments on linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118) using linear_list_comments for any additional specifics Hana may have added.
- Retrieve QuickBooks bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria on DocNumber: "HM-2026-Q1-0042" or vendor: "Heartland Movers") or quickbooks_get_bill. CORRECTION HM-2026-Q1-0042 is a vendor BILL (payable), not a customer invoice, and lives in quickbooks.bills.json, not quickbooks.invoices.json. Two bill records exist: bill_heartland_q1_2026 (as-submitted by Heartland, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 total, 8 lines × $1,600) and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (MoveOps-annotated hold version, DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD, same $12,800 with 5 [VERIFIED] lines and 3 DISPUTED lines for Marchetti/Osei/Venkatesh). Use the bill to anchor the $12,800 total and the 8-line structure. IMPORTANT: Kevin Tran and Priya Nair do NOT appear as distinct additional-gap line items inside the bill itself Kevin Tran shows as a [VERIFIED] full-service relocation line, and Priya Nair is not present in this bill's 8 line items at all. Hana's Kevin Tran storage and Priya Nair shuttle findings are narrative discoveries documented in Linear issue #118 based on ops notes, authorization records, and coordinator notes not on new QuickBooks line items. The bill read is still needed for the $12,800 anchor and the 3-disputed-vs-5-verified structure that grounds Chloe's baseline number before layering Hana's additional findings.
- Search Linear for Alejandro's consolidated vendor margin dataset ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor margin" or "Q3 dataset" or "cost impact" or "margin" or similar). Find: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (ticket #214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM", assigned to Alejandro Fuentes, priority 1, due April 16 2026, created April 14 2026). Retrieve the issue detail using linear_get_issue. The description explicitly enumerates blockers: "Airtable vendor assignment fields are incomplete or inconsistent across active moves. QuickBooks bills are not consistently tagged to relocation records or client accounts. CRM upcoming volume is separate from operations data and difficult to map by vendor exposure." The ticket has state_id null (not Done), meaning the dataset is still In Progress and the forward-looking margin model has not yet landed as a finished artifact. This is the blocker Chloe must flag to Marcus.
- Search Slack and emails for any Alejandro updates on the margin model using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Alejandro" or "margin" or "cost impact" or "Q3 dataset" or similar) and search_emails. Confirm no landed draft exists Alejandro has only posted directional commentary (e.g., Vectral 32% blended margin, scenario analysis) but no final margin impact numbers have landed. This IS a blocker on the memo and must be flagged to Marcus.
- Search Slack for Elena's Tuesday directive in #executive and any prior vendor-crisis discussion using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "vendor strategy" or "Lakeline" or "Heartland" or "Swift" or "capacity" or similar) on C004 (#executive) and C006 (#operations).
- Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com. Also look up the coordinators on the exposed moves Javier Morales, Blessing Okafor, and Suki Patel using contacts_search_contacts to ground the coordinator-assignment findings.
- Search Linear for the vendor strategy memo tracking ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "CEO memo" or "backup capacity" or similar). Find: linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (ticket #318, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options", owner: Marcus Thorne, priority 1, due April 29 2026, created April 24 2026, status In Progress). Retrieve full details and existing comments using linear_get_issue and linear_list_comments before adding a new comment.
- Send the backup-capacity note email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"], subject: short backup-capacity note title, content: body below). Content must cover: (1) Lakeline is the ONLY backup vendor Chloe probed no other outreach exists; (2) Lakeline coverage footprint (Midwest/Southeast, specific states) AND the explicit West Coast / national-replacement gap; (3) named exposed in-progress moves with coordinator assignments AND vendor-lock status inferred cross-service at minimum Rachel Dominguez (LA → Portland, Javier Morales) and Kevin Okafor (Phoenix → Portland, Blessing Okafor), both with no main vendor committed in QuickBooks bills or Swift's surcharge shipment list; (4) Heartland 20% Q3 increase explicitly tied to unresolved Q1 dispute HM-2026-Q1-0042 relevant to negotiation posture; (5) Swift 15% + $500 DOT surcharge already live on 4 in-progress shipments (Raj Patel, Emilia Cruz, Jordan Ekwueme, Nora Bennett); (6) Hana's ADDITIONAL Heartland Q1 findings from Linear #118 beyond the original three disputed moves documented in VRECON-2026-Q1-HM specifically: Kevin Tran has 2 days of storage billed without signed contract authorization, and Priya Nair has a shuttle fee mismatch versus origin access notes and approved scope. These are narrative findings from Hana's April 14 reconciliation review, not new QuickBooks line items, and they indicate the real exposure is broader than the $4,800 / three-move number Marcus may be working from; (7) Alejandro's cost-impact model (Linear #214) has NOT landed and remains In Progress with blockers on Airtable vendor assignment fields, QuickBooks bill tagging, and CRM volume mapping this is a blocker on the memo and Marcus needs to know now.
- Add a comment on the vendor strategy memo Linear ticket (linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36, ticket #318) using linear_create_comment documenting Chloe's full findings: Lakeline coverage + hard gap, exposed moves with coordinators + vendor-lock status (inferred cross-service), Heartland Q1-dispute tie-in on rate increase, Swift already-live $500 DOT surcharge on 4 named shipments, Hana's additional Q1 findings (Kevin Tran storage + Priya Nair shuttle from Linear #118 narrative), and the Alejandro Linear #214 dataset-not-landed blocker. The comment must surface the findings so Hana, Alejandro, and David can build on them in the shared ticket.
- Post a heads-up in the #operations Slack channel (C006) using conversations_add_message. Content should call out the specific exposed in-progress moves by name (Rachel Dominguez, Kevin Okafor, any others) and their coordinators, noting that these relocations are on routes with NO vendor backstop if Heartland or Swift walks so the coordinators on those moves are aware of the vendor-risk posture while the strategy memo is being finalized.
